candidates.stmt:
Nadja is a designer who has lived in Lewisham for nearly a decade.

Having grown up in social housing, Nadja is committed to advocating for a higher standard of living for everyone, especially those in rented accommodation. She is standing to ensure Bellingham receives the attention, investment, and representation it deserves.

Nadja believes that big changes come from small, consistent steps, starting at the community level. Local council decisions should be guided by those who live in the area, as they understand its needs best and should have agency over their own communities.

I’m a Lewisham resident and college governor for a group of special needs schools. I want to get the basics right for Bellingham: clean and safe streets, decent homes and real chances for young people.

Stuart believes Bellingham has been overlooked for too long and is committed to changing that. Inspired by a bold, people-first vision for Green politics, he is standing to deliver real action for residents, with engaged, accountable representation that puts the community first.

I’m standing as an independent candidate in Bellingham.
For the past 3 years, I’ve been taking action locally — collecting over 1,300 bags of litter, reporting fly-tipping, restoring neglected spaces, and creating a community garden in Bell Green.
I didn’t start this to run for office. I started because standards had slipped and I care about the area my children are growing up in.
But after years of doing the work, I’ve seen what’s possible — and what isn’t working.
That’s why I’m putting my name on the ballot.
I believe Bellingham deserves:
* Cleaner streets and properly maintained spaces
* Real accountability when things go wrong
* Thoughtful development that improves our area
* Support for residents who want to make a difference locally
I’m not backed by a political party.
I’m focused on Bellingham.
If elected, I’ll bring the same energy, persistence and practical action into the council that I’ve already shown on our streets.

I'm a Lewisham GP and resident of Blackheath. I feel proud to be a resident of the borough of Lewisham and to be bringing up my children here.

As a GP, I have a deep understanding of our community and its needs, as well as the inequalities that plague it. I want to use this understanding and the skills acquired as a doctor to represent my neighbours.

I'm passionate about social and environmental justice, and I’m in awe of the way that Lewisham residents have fought for justice,  historically and now. I would be proud to be part of this.

I’ve represented Blackheath ward since July 2024. I’ve lived here for over 30 years and brought my children up here. As a lawyer and advice worker I enjoy meeting residents and making sure their questions are answered.

l've been a resident of Blackheath for a decade and of London for almost 30 years. I'm an operations professional and an advocate for a fairer and greener society.
As a mother who has lived in two Lewisham wards, I'm tuned into their challenges and potential and would be proud to represent my neighbours in Blackheath and beyond.
I'm standing with the Greens because we need bold and different politics!

I'm a committed trade unionist and socialist with experience working in the NHS, local authorities and universities. I'm passionate about providing accessible spaces for disabled people and looking after carers, who do so much for society.
Blackheath was my first true "home" in London, the first place where I lived for more than a year due to moving around so much in my teens and early twenties. I spent 6 fantastic years in Blackheath and love the area.
I'm standing for the Greens to help build a better future!

Chris first became involved with the party in Orpington, where he was elected as a Councillor.

During his 20 years representing Orpington ward, the Council group grew to 25 and Chris became the only non-Tory leader of Bromley Council between 1998 and 2001. He fought the Orpington parliamentary seat several times missing out by 269 votes in 2001.

In 2006 Chris moved to Lewisham where he was elected to represent Blackheath ward and was involved in building the Lib Dems' largest ever representation on Lewisham Council. 

Chris has a record of campaigning in London, particularly on health issues and for better commuter train services. Since earlier in 2020 Chris has been working for the NHS on an assignment linked to Covid19 and he has seen first-hand how the health service has responded the epidemic.

He is also the Liberal Democrat candidate for mayor.
I grew up in Blackheath and taught in London comprehensive schools for nearly a decade. I’m passionate about education, development of skills and good jobs for young people in the community.

I’m committed to keeping Blackheath special. I’ll strengthen community safety and work with residents to improve road safety. I want to make housing more affordable, support our local businesses, expand opportunities for young people and keep residents at the heart of decision making.

Ade has lived in Brockley for three years, where her life in Lewisham began. She is passionate about ensuring communities across Lewisham win together, and believes that’s the Green Party way.

By day, Ade works in the tech industry and also serves as a director of a CIC exploring the impact of data-centric technologies on Black people and other marginalised groups. Deeply invested in the local community,

“I am invested in making Brockley, and Lewisham as a whole, affordable and sustainable enough that others who love it as much as I do, will be able to stay and continue to make it the amazing place it is.”

Rotimi’s family has been part of Brockley and Lewisham since the Windrush generation. He is passionate about community building and making the neighbourhood safer for all. His career spans architecture, youth work, data analytics, archiving, and currently recorded music, where he has advocated for safer workplaces for workers, supported youth pathways into the arts, and helped develop educational resources on British history.

"I care about protecting social housing, providing services for our youth, and making the neighbourhood cleaner and safer."

Your council should always have residents’ interests at heart, and I’ll work to deliver practical, people-first policies for Brockley.

Lewisham has been El’s home since she was two, and it has shaped who she is. El is passionate about protecting council housing, supporting local businesses, keeping streets safe and clean, and making our borough greener and more accessible.

Local decisions should serve residents, not work against them. The Green Party offers practical, people-first solutions. El is ready to be a strong, positive voice for Brockley.

“I’m standing in Brockley because I believe politics should be fair, caring, and powered by the community.”

Briony has lived in Lewisham her whole life and is now raising her own family in the borough. She moved to Catford South five years ago and has enjoyed becoming part of its vibrant and active community, while also seeing firsthand the challenges residents face.

Proud to call Catford home, Briony believes that with the right care and investment, it can be a safe, clean and welcoming place for everyone. She is committed to making sure the area gets the investment and future its community deserves.

Briony is standing for the Green Party to bring meaningful change to Lewisham and offer a clear alternative to the status quo.

Rashid has been a Lewisham resident for 27 years and has seen first-hand how the borough has been let down by decades of neglect and underinvestment. Growing up on a council estate and being given the opportunity of higher education shaped his belief that everyone, regardless of background, deserves the chance to thrive.

“I’m standing because I want to serve my community, my neighbours who, like me, have been let down by our political class.”

Miki was born in London and moved to Lewisham 12 years ago, where she has been happily based ever since.

She is passionate about tackling air pollution, having been diagnosed with asthma and advised by her GP that it was linked to traffic in London. She is particularly concerned about issues such as motor idling and wider environmental and social challenges affecting local communities.

Miki is an enthusiastic cyclist and runner, and works as a theatre technician. She is committed to helping create a healthier, fairer environment for everyone in Lewisham.

Catford resident Diana Cashin is one of many committed campaigners working to improve the lives of Catford residents. In the 23 years she has lived in Catford Diana has become known as an active campaigner on issues that affect the area, working closely with residents’ associations.

Recent campaigns have been for safety improvements at Catford station, pedestrian phasing at a dangerous Brownhill Road junction, and better services for social and private tenants.

Diana said “I have become increasingly disillusioned with the way that Lewisham Council overrides the views of the people who live here, organises token ‘consultations’ and sends out self-congratulatory propaganda at our expense. I have long been a member of the Lib Dems and am now looking forward to fighting for council seats.   I will challenge the one-party state that Lewisham has become and speak up for the people who want to see a better Catford.”

Kate has lived in Catford South for over 30 years and has been an active member of her local residents’ association for over 20 years.

She loves the diversity and cosmopolitan nature of the area and believes this is what attracts people to put down roots and build a community here.

Kate’s campaigns include: • working with community groups to ensure better regulation of Houses in Multiple Occupation for the benefit of all.

• working with the Safer Neighbourhood Team for over 10 years to increase police presence in the area. Thanks to her work with the team, the Team now publish their patrol walks.

• pressing the Council to use its powers to improve the rate of fly-tipping and dumping prosecutions.   Kate says "Local residents deserve better – someone who will stand up for their concerns. The Council says it will consult with residents but does it listen? No-one denies that Catford needs improving but are the council taking notice of the concerns of the people who live close by? Or will they continue to act as they did with the Low Traffic Neighbourhood scheme and impose their will? I will be a voice for Catford South, I will be visible, I will listen to your views and concerns and work for you"

**Across Lewisham, people feel the same: we deserve better.**

We’re tired of a system that protects billionaires while working people struggle.Our rivers are polluted, bills keep rising, housing is insecure, and decisions are made without our communities.

**But alongside the frustration, there is hope.**

Something special is happening in Lewisham. The Green Party came second in the last mayoral election and our support is growing fast. On 7 May, we can elect a Green Mayor and change how Lewisham is run.

**That’s why I’m standing to be the next Mayor of Lewisham.**

I’m standing because I believe in a fairer Lewisham. I grew up in a single-parent family, supported by a strong community and always understood the need to fight injustice. After studying at Goldsmiths, I worked for an anti-racism charity, before serving as a Lewisham councillor for four years.

Since I was elected, I’ve secured pedestrianisation on dangerous roads, protected small businesses from closure, secured investment for community groups, fought the far-right, demanded safer housing for residents and lobbied in support of refugees.

Labour’s complicity with the genocide in Gaza and inaction on cost of living crisis has let us all down. That’s why I joined the Green Party: the party of social justice, real democracy, and protecting our environment.

**My 5 priorities as Mayor**

1. Build homes for people not profit. Safe and secure at prices people can actually afford.

2. Deliver cleaner, safer streets and more protected cycle lanes

3. Protect public assets from sell-off, through community partnerships

4. Unlock more funding for youth services

5. Stop wasting millions on expensive consultants and failed projects

**On 7 May, you have a choice: more of the same, 
or a Green Mayor who puts people first.**

Together, we can fix what’s broken.

In solidarity,

Cllr Liam Shrivastava

Natalie’s work as a journalist has seen her reporting on social justice and environmental crises around the world. She wants to use her experiences to help hold Lewisham Council to account and fight for every resident of the ward.

Aaron was born in Lewisham Hospital and has lived in the borough his whole life.

Aaron’s currently training to be a solicitor. He wants to focus on helping residents secure their fundamental human right to good quality, affordable housing.

As a councillor, Aaron will lobby for faster repairs and better communication between the Council and renters. He will put pressure on the Cabinet and the Mayor to ensure that the interests of big money never come before the interests of Lewisham residents.

I know the difference Labour makes.

I grew up in a town hit hard by the closure of the steelworks and Tory cuts. I personally benefited from Labour's investment in schools, reconnecting us with a long-awaited train station, and supporting families in need. That opportunity meant I became the first in my family to go to university, where I served two terms as Students' Union President at the University of Greenwich, representing more than 30,000 students across the South East.

Those same values of fairness and opportunity drive me now in Lewisham. Outside of work and the Party, I volunteer as Vice-Chair with a Ladywell-based housing and grant giving charity that provides homes for those in need later in life.

I'm standing to be your Crofton Park councillor—to put opportunity, fairness, and community at the heart of local life.

**My commitment to you in Crofton Park:**

- **Focused on local priorities:** I will build on the work of your outgoing councillors by improving road safety, tackling fly-tipping and street cleanliness, and working with police and council officers to reduce crime, speeding and antisocial behaviour.
- **Accessible and accountable:** I will be an active Crofton Park councillor. I will be visible, present, and responsive to residents.
- **Rebuilding trust:** Politics only works when people are part of it. I will listen, communicate clearly, and involve the community in decisions, making sure choices are understood and shared openly.

**My commitment to the people of Lewisham:**

- **Housing:** Champion genuinely affordable homes for families on all incomes, from those who need social housing to those working hard but still priced out, so everyone can build a future in Lewisham.
- **Cleaner, safer streets:** Tackle fly-tipping head-on with stronger enforcement, better waste services, and community partnerships. Clean streets and pride in our borough are the basics we all deserve.
- **Fair funding for Lewisham:** Lead the case for a fair deal fr

Heather is an unusual type of software engineer – she distrusts computers and loves talking to people!

She wants to apply her skills to scrutinise the systems the council uses to run its services. Not in pursuit of cost savings or to make cuts, but to make sure they are set up in a technically literate way and are running well for residents.

People with skills like hers must offer them to our communities. Local people deserve functioning local services, and it’s very clear that they aren’t getting them at the moment.

Hau-Yu was the first London councillor to defect to the Greens in 2025, after facing political persecution for standing up for Palestine.

She saw up close Labour’s embrace of racism, fascism, and transphobia – growth with zero wellbeing or social justice indicators, fuelling genocide and war. The Greens stood by her, and now she’s fighting back against those injustices.

As an anti-oppression campaigner, Hau-Yu cares about protecting and empowering our communities. She’ll listen to you and fight to support Deptford. We’re in this together.

Her family are migrants from Hong Kong, China and Malaysia – some have lived in Lewisham since the 90s. This campaign is for all of us.

Daniel grew up in South East London and has lived in Lewisham for the last three years.

Working as an architect, he’s seen the impact secure and affordable homes can have for people, and is passionate about the delivery of social housing across the borough.

Daniel wants to work to ensure that Lewisham is safer, cleaner and greener, and that improvements and developments put local communities first.

Are you sick of seeing the rich get richer while the rest of us struggle to make ends meet? Are you tired of mainstream political parties turning a blind eye to war, injustice and the decline of working people’s quality of life and public services? 

As a proud resident of Deptford, I have experienced firsthand the challenges facing the people of our constituency. I have fought to organise better conditions for workplaces, joined actions to resist deportations and evictions and campaigned in solidarity with Palestine. As your candidate for Lewisham North, I want to stand with you in order to fight for a better quality of life, defend our communities and stand up for what is right. 

Although I am standing in this election to be your voice, I believe that we must campaign for the needs and aspirations of working people every day. I believe that where people get organised and fight, we can win. I am proud to be a member of the local campaigning Communist Party in Lewisham. Vote for me, your Communist candidate, and send a message to mainstream politicians that we need radical change today!

As your Labour Parliamentary candidate, I believe in investing in public services, championing quality education and healthcare, and building a fair and prosperous economy. These values reflect our collective vision for a society where everyone has equal opportunities to thrive.

If elected as your Member of Parliament, I will prioritise the needs of our community above all else. Whether it’s advocating for increased funding for the NHS, improving public transport, or better educational opportunities for children, I will be a relentless voice for positive change.

Our local businesses are the backbone of our community, and I am committed to supporting and celebrating them. In the face of challenges such as rising energy costs and rents I will work tirelessly to support their continued success.

I will establish a constituency office that is easily accessible to you, where my team and I will be available to address any concerns or issues you may face.

Your voice matters, and I will make it heard in parliament. Together, let us build a future where opportunities abound, where the cost-of-living crisis is addressed, and where everyone has a fair start in life.

Daniel is a proud Downham local, raised here and educated here. He understands the people, parks and what makes this community special. He’s standing to help make Downham cleaner, safer and greener for everyone.

Daniel wants to work with the community to tackle issues like litter and fly-tipping, protect our green spaces, improve local services and make sure residents’ voices are heard at Lewisham Council.
Committed to ending austerity for ordinary people, improving living conditions and ensuring our children have a future home to look live in.

A migrant from Turkey, Leni has called the UK home for 18 years, and Lewisham for 8. For over a decade, she has worked in communications roles for charities, campaigning for the rights of migrants, refugees and LGBTIQA+ communities.

Leni continues to volunteer with LGBTIQA+ organisations and has led campaigns challenging unfair immigration policies. Leni is standing for the Greens for social justice, climate action and a fairer system for everyone.

Dylan works as a market trader and hears every day about the challenges local people are facing with the rising cost of living. Through these conversations, he sees just how urgently Lewisham needs representatives who will stand up for working people.

He believes there is a huge opportunity for the Green Party to offer something different from the tired old politics of Labour and to bring real change to the borough.

Lewisham is Dylan’s home, and he wants others to feel at home here too. If elected, he will work hard to make sure Lewisham is an affordable, diverse and beautiful place for everyone to live.

Fergus is a research and insights professional with expertise in data analysis, storytelling and trend analysis. Originally from Sussex, he has lived in Lewisham for over a decade, moving to the borough after university and quickly becoming part of the local community. He now lives with his partner and their two children, both born at Lewisham Hospital.

After time away from party politics, Fergus was inspired to get involved again by the energy and ideas coming from the Green Party. He is standing to help revitalise hope, tackle inequality and build more inclusive, sustainable communities in Lewisham.

I am honoured delighted to be the Liberal Democratic candidate for the Lewisham West and Penge Constituency where my family and I have lived in Forest Hill for 15 years.

I am proud to be a Liberal Democrat and I have been a party member since the mid 80's.

I was a Lewisham Councillor on Lewisham Council from 2006-2010 and I was the Lewisham and Greenwich Constituency candidate at the London Assembly elections in 2012.

I am the Chair of the Board of trustees at Wide Horizons Outdoor Education Trust a national charity that provides life-changing outdoor education to over 50,000 children and young people each year. Wide Horizons works with many schools in both Lewisham and Bromley. I have been volunteering with Wide Horizons since 2006.

I am passionate about my local community and working to make improvements. As an elected councilor I helped to Save Forest Hill Pools and to get them rebuilt. I opposed the closure of Forest Hill Library and after the decision was taken I have worked to support the community Library where I sit on the management committee .
More recently I have been campaigning to keep our direct train services to East Croydon and to hold Thames Water to account for the number of large water leaks in the local area.

I am passionate about the environment about Young people and education and on improving housing.

I am fighting to oppose a hard Brexit and to keep Britain open, tolerant and a united place to live.

I believe that Lewisham West and Penge deserves a hard working MP who is committed to helping local residents.

Cormac moved to Lewisham with his wife twelve years ago to raise their family in the borough where she was born. He values the strong sense of community and the people who make Lewisham such a special place to live.

He believes the council should do more to support residents and respond to community needs. Cormac is committed to championing a council that is democratic, responsive and enabling.

He brings a wealth of experience, having served as a school governor and youth club trustee, and worked on delivering council-rent housing. He is standing for the Green Party to help make Lewisham Council work better for everyone.

Sylbourne came to the UK from Jamaica in 1992 and has spent the past three decades building a life, a family, and a voice for his community in Lewisham.

An entrepreneur, lawyer, content creator and motivational speaker, Sylbourne has dedicated his career to inspiring people, connecting communities, and being a catalyst for change. He founded the “Politics and You” initiative to educate and empower young people to engage with political decision-making — because, as he believes, the leaders of tomorrow must be equipped to shape their future today.

In 2015, Sylbourne launched the Sylbourne Sydial Show, a thought-provoking talk show showcasing ordinary people doing extraordinary things. During the Covid pandemic, he pioneered “Walk & Talk Motivational Moments”, going live daily from the park to encourage people to stay positive and keep moving forward.

Proudly married with two children, Sylbourne also serves on the board of his local church.

His priorities for Grove Park are clear:

-Safer, cleaner streets where residents feel proud and secure.
    -Support for young people, giving them the skills and confidence to succeed.
    -Revitalising our high street, backing small businesses and driving regeneration.
    -A stronger voice for our community on Lewisham Council.

“I consider my life a platform — unapologetic in its values, determined to connect people, and driven to make a difference,” Sylbourne said. “It would be my honour to represent Grove Park.”

Eleonora emigrated from Italy to London in 2020. She did a master’s in education policy, worked in secondary schools as support staff, and is now a research student in education and youth well-being at King’s College – where she also teaches.

Eleonora is standing for the Greens in Hither Green because in them she found a like-minded community of people whose actions reflect care about socio-economic justice for all.

Andy plans to spend the rest of his life in Hither Green and is committed to helping build a council that truly reflects and responds to the people who live there.

His career has been rooted in public service, working with non-profits to identify problems, uncover opportunities, and turn ideas into practical change. Listening, problem-solving, and helping organisations deliver on their mission are central to his work.

Andy is standing for the Green Party to ensure Hither Green has engaged, accountable representation that is rooted in the community.

Arron has been a local Hither Green resident for three years. He has a master's in Data Science and Statistics from the University of Bath, and has experience of working in local government. For the last three years, Arron has been self-employed running his own technology business alongside being a retail manager.

Arron is standing in Hither Green because he is passionate about making a real and substantive difference to his local area, especially for young and vulnerable people.

Arron's Four Key Priorities for Hither Green are:
•⁠  ⁠Clean neighbourhoods.
•⁠  ⁠Safe streets.
•⁠  ⁠Strong local business.
•⁠  ⁠Support local communities.

Peckham is an amazing place, full of energy and entrepreneurial spirit. There is so much to be proud of but so much more potential too - and I want to help harness that.    A vote for me on July 4 is a vote for backing our incredible local businesses, a vote for fighting for better transport links, a vote for campaigning for safer streets, and – as the foundation for all of that - a vote for a strong economy that protects household budgets by keeping inflation and taxes low.

Bunmi lives in Brockley and has lived in Lewisham for many years, her children have grown up here. Her passion is to be a voice for the people of the community, especially members who are often overlooked, to be able to serve and support people, to empower local residents. Bunmi says: "I’m standing in Catford South as I believe that it’s time for a change. The one-party Labour council has become complacent: it’s time for new voices to represent this ward and bring about progressive change.

Ese Erheriene grew up in a working-class household in inner city London. From there she learned hard work, drive and compassion. The daughter of Nigerian immigrants, she also learned that Labour was the only party that could be relied upon.

She is standing to be Woking's first Labour MP because it is a town full of hardworking people who deserve to have their voices heard in the halls of Westminster. She cares deeply about the town and its communities --- and she wants to use her experience to help address the areas where Woking has been failed by 14 years of Tory austerity, and to champion the areas where it is leading the way.

Labour values have driven her throughout her career and led her to becoming a financial journalist, where she uncovered financial corruption and wrote about pro-democracy movements.

As a board member for a mental health charity, she developed a peer support programme during the pandemic that supported vulnerable people and helped them find community.

As a local councillor, she has a strong track record of being a voice for the voiceless. She brought ThamesWater executives before the committee to demand that the cost of their spiralling debts not be passed onto consumers.

She has continually fought to resolve complex casework, ranging from meeting emergency housing needs, to supporting vulnerable residents with mobility issues, to tackling serious breaches of environmental regulations. She is currently a cabinet advisor with the portfolio of improving health inequalities for women, through which she has called on the government to launch a full statutory enquiry into maternity safety.

And during the Cost of Living Crisis, Ese raised hundreds of pounds for people struggling to heat their homes by trekking to Everest Base Camp.

Laura is a long-time Green Party supporter who has lived in South East London for over 20 years. She values Lewisham’s vibrant community and is passionate about supporting and strengthening local neighbourhoods.

She believes community wellbeing is essential and sees Lee Green as a place with huge potential. Laura is committed to listening to residents and local businesses, working with them to address the issues that matter most and improve quality of life.

Working in the creative industries as a make-up artist, Laura brings a collaborative and people-focused approach. She is standing for the Green Party to help make Lee Green a safer, cleaner and greener place for everyone, and to bring hope and positive change for the future.

Lizzie Fox has been a personal Assistant for over 26 years. She has lived in South East London all her life, and for the last five years in Lee Green. She loves getting involved in the local community and is a keen (albeit not very good) runner. She has helped to set up two local parkruns, often volunteers with them and has raised money for charity from her running. 

Lizzie’s interest in local politics was sparked by the injustice of so many people in the ward being ignored by their councillors and so she decided to step up.

James is a Dad to two children and a dog, and has been resident in Lewisham since 1997 and in the Lee Green Ward since 1999. He volunteers for VSL (Volunteer Services Lewisham) as a driver, has been a constant presence in local vaccination centres since Jan 2021 and regularly gets on his cycle to deliver for 2 local foodbanks – AFRIL and Lewisham (Hope Centre). 

For a day job, he founded marketing company www.kingpincomms.com in 1997. He sold his shares in March 2020 and is now active in a number of businesses either as a Non-Exec or directly funding and managing them. 

He hates injustice and thinks the LTN needs to go back to the drawing board.

Ruth cares deeply about the everyday issues people face – from dangerous traffic near schools to fly-tipping and housing concerns.

She’s standing to fight for strong local services, safer streets, and practical climate action that benefits New Cross Gate now and in the future.

“I believe everyone deserves the opportunity to live well, and councils can help to make that happen.”

Born in the Dominican Republic, Wellington is a Spanish citizen who has lived in London for 11 years, 8 of them in Lewisham.

As a Latin American and first-generation immigrant, he’s intimately familiar with the challenges our community faces: council negligence, tenant abuses, and lack of support for social housing and young people with disabilities like his daughter.

“I want to be the voice of the Latin American community in Lewisham, advocating for rights, improving access to services, promoting Spanish in schools, fostering Latin culture, health, employment, and transparency.”

As a deaf person and carer, Justin understands the challenges disabled people and SEN parents face. He’s committed to fighting for inclusivity and access in Perry Vale and across Lewisham.

Justin will work hard to make Perry Vale greener, cleaner, safer and more accessible for everyone.

After many years of living and working in South London, Lewisham is very much my home. My friends and family are here and I’m the proud father of a 14yr old daughter who grew up here. 
I’m a professional campaigner and advocate for justice, equality and inclusion of communities in decision-making locally and internationally. 
I am standing to help transform the failing political system, to revitalise hope and achieve real change towards greater equality and social justice, more inclusive communities and a more sustainable environment.

I support all of the pledges in the Lewisham Labour Manifesto. You can read a full copy at: www.lewishamlabour.com/manifesto

In addition I also hope to continue representing well the interests of ALL people living in Perry Vale ward on Lewisham Council, for the benefit of the many and the place where we all live.

I have lived in Lewisham since I was 5 years old and seen many changes over the years. I now wish to champion issues for Bellingham ward and for Lewisham Borough.

I want to make Lewisham a better place for everyone. I want everyone to have a fair chance and opportunity to reach their full potential.

I own and run my own environmental services business in Lewisham providing local employment for 3 people.

Homes For Lewisham - There is an overwhelming need for affordable housing in Lewisham whether it be to rent or to buy. I want to work towards affordable housing for all. I will insist that new developments include genuinely affordable homes for rent.
Lib Dem-run Sutton has set up a development company to build the homes that are needed in their borough. This is something I believe we can successfully emulate in Lewisham. 

I want to ensure the council uses more Empty Management Dwelling Orders on properties left empty for long periods and has a plan of action to tackle rogue landlords.

It is a fact that poor housing and families in bed & breakfast accommodation leads to poor educational attainment and poor health outcomes. I believe that improving housing is key to improving the well being of people in the borough.

Focus on Education - As a borough we have a poor record of achievement for secondary schools which is reflected in Ofsted reports. I want to see an increased focus on the quality of our education services. 

Government figures that measure progress from Key Stage 2 to the end of Key Stage 4 rate four Lewisham secondary schools as Well Below Average. This places Lewisham as the second worst borough in the whole of Greater London. Lewisham Council is failing to give our secondary schools the support they need. This problem is symptomatic of the complacency that has set in under Labour in Lewisham.
There are people who have experience of turning schools around. As your councillor I will work with my council colleagues and stakeholders to

Samantha Gardiner is a life-long resident of the borough of Lewisham, from many generations living here. She is now proudly raising her own family in Catford.

Samantha is a children's NHS nurse with a wealth of experience in child and adolescent mental health. Samantha has lots of experience working alongside vulnerable people and advocatong for parents and families to get their voices heard.

Samantha has strong ties in the community, being a regular contributor to community litter picks as well as being an active member of the local school charity committee, running community events and raising much-needed funds to support pupils and families.

Samantha is a committed green, and is passionate about sustainability, protecting our planet and a fierce advocate for social justice.

As a Catford resident herself, Samantha understnads the issues that are facing residents here. She is passionate about investing in Catford and making it somewhere to be proud of.

Corin has lived in Lewisham for 14 years and works as a Software Engineer for a national charity. His background is in building computer systems for local authorities and public bodies.

As a father of a child at a Lewisham comprehensive school, Corin is particularly concerned to make sure kids and young people in our borough have every opportunity and can thrive with safer streets and clean air.

Corin has been involved with the Green Party for over 20 years in one way or another and is committed to its values and the philosophy that environmental and social justice have to go hand-in-hand. Corin is looking forward to working with and for the people of Sydenham to realise this ideal.

Tanya is committed to supporting people, guided by principles of justice and kindness. Her career has focused on developing and supporting others, often through challenging times in their lives.

Locally, she has worked with neighbours to secure the right to manage their block, standing up against neglect and unfair costs. She has also volunteered as a school governor and been an active campaigner with LGBTQIA+ football fan groups.

Tanya is dedicated to putting in the work to create positive change and is standing for the Green Party to serve the Sydenham community and help make a real difference across Lewisham.

Originally from Canada, Markus has lived in Sydenham for 12 years. He’s passionate about collective people power and community, particularly how it can bring about real change. He knows how important it is for people to be involved in the way their local community is organised and fostered.

A musician and teacher, Markus has curated and hosted a community music night on the high street for the past four years, been involved with the Sydenham Arts Trail, and supported other local events throughout Sydenham and the wider area.

He plans to improve green spaces, access to local services, and social housing. He wants to help make Sydenham & Lewisham a safe place for everyone.

“I’m running because I love Sydenham and the people here. I want to make sure that our community is fully represented and that everyone’s voices are heard. I’ve spent years writing songs about hope, and I believe that with the Green Party, I can help bring those hopes to life.”

The basis for my political views is that we are not separate from nature but an integral part of it. I believe that every person has equal value within nature, equal rights to share in its fruits and equal responsibility to protect it. I am running for the Green Party because it is the party that most accurately represents this view.

For a long time, I worked in the field of child mental health as a researcher and evaluator. I now run my own business as a bodyworker and treat a broad community in Lewisham for the alleviation of pain, stress, and other mental and physical health issues. I am now in my final year of training as a trauma therapist and I also hold a BSc in Public Policy. My focus politically, personally and professionally is the improvement of people’s wellbeing: listening to and understanding their problems and working with them to find the best solutions to them.

I grew up in Telegraph Hill, I went to primary and secondary school there and now regularly take my own children to the play club in the park. Being part of the vibrant community there helped to shape my world view of valuing equality and fairness and celebrating diversity. It would be an honour to represent the people of the ward and see the community thrive.
